9:[["$","title",null,{"children":"Erasure Coding Principles"}],["$","meta",null,{"property":"og:title","content":"Erasure Coding Principles"}],["$","meta",null,{"property":"og:description","content":"RustFS, as a new-generation distributed object storage system, demonstrates unique advantages in cloud storage through innovative architectural design and memory safety features. One of its core innovations is the deep application of Reed-Solomon Erasure Coding."}],["$","meta",null,{"name":"description","content":"RustFS, as a new-generation distributed object storage system, demonstrates unique advantages in cloud storage through innovative architectural design and memory safety features. One of its core innovations is the deep application of Reed-Solomon Erasure Coding."}],[["$","$b","0",{"children":[["$","meta",null,{"property":"og:image","content":"https://docs.rustfs.com/concepts/principle/erasure-coding.webp"}],["$","meta",null,{"property":"og:image:width","content":"1200"}],["$","meta",null,{"property":"og:image:height","content":"630"}],["$","meta",null,{"property":"twitter:card","content":"summary_large_image"}]]}]]]
a:["$","$Lc",null,{"toc":[{"depth":2,"url":"#core-algorithm-and-application-scope","title":"Core Algorithm and Application Scope"},{"depth":3,"url":"#11-distributed-storage-systems-such-as-rustfs","title":"1.1. Distributed Storage Systems (such as RustFS)"},{"depth":3,"url":"#12-communication-transmission","title":"1.2. Communication Transmission"},{"depth":3,"url":"#13-digital-media-storage","title":"1.3. Digital Media Storage"},{"depth":2,"url":"#basic-concepts-of-erasure-coding","title":"Basic Concepts of Erasure Coding"},{"depth":3,"url":"#21-evolution-of-storage-redundancy","title":"2.1 Evolution of Storage Redundancy"},{"depth":3,"url":"#22-core-parameter-definitions","title":"2.2 Core Parameter Definitions"},{"depth":2,"url":"#mathematical-principles-of-reed-solomon-codes","title":"Mathematical Principles of Reed-Solomon Codes"},{"depth":3,"url":"#31-finite-field-galois-field-construction","title":"3.1 Finite Field (Galois Field) Construction"},{"depth":3,"url":"#32-encoding-matrix-construction","title":"3.2 Encoding Matrix Construction"},{"depth":3,"url":"#33-encoding-process","title":"3.3 Encoding Process"},{"depth":2,"url":"#engineering-implementation-in-rustfs","title":"Engineering Implementation in RustFS"},{"depth":3,"url":"#41-data-sharding-strategy","title":"4.1 Data Sharding Strategy"},{"depth":3,"url":"#42-parallel-encoding-optimization","title":"4.2 Parallel Encoding Optimization"},{"depth":3,"url":"#43-decoding-recovery-process","title":"4.3 Decoding Recovery Process"}],"children":[["$","$Ld",null,{"children":"Erasure Coding Principles"}],["$","$Le",null,{"className":"mb-0","children":"RustFS, as a new-generation distributed object storage system, demonstrates unique advantages in cloud storage through innovative architectural design and memory safety features. One of its core innovations is the deep application of Reed-Solomon Erasure Coding."}],["$","div",null,{"className":"flex flex-row gap-2 items-center border-b pt-2 pb-6","children":[["$","$Lf",null,{"markdownUrl":"/concepts/principle/erasure-coding.md"}],["$","$L10",null,{"markdownUrl":"/concepts/principle/erasure-coding.md","githubUrl":"https://github.com/rustfs/docs.rustfs.com/blob/main/content/concepts/principle/erasure-coding.md"}]]}],["$","$L11",null,{"children":[["$","$L12",null,{"as":"h2","id":"core-algorithm-and-application-scope","children":"Core Algorithm and Application Scope"}],"\n",["$","p",null,{"children":["Reed-Solomon Code (RS Code) is an erasure code based on finite field algebraic structures. Due to its ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"efficient data recovery capability"}]," and ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"flexible redundancy configuration"}],", it is widely used in multiple fields. Below, we detail its core application scenarios from two dimensions: technical fields and practical applications:"]}],"\n",["$","$L12",null,{"as":"h3","id":"11-distributed-storage-systems-such-as-rustfs","children":"1.1. Distributed Storage Systems (such as RustFS)"}],"\n",["$","ul",null,{"children":["\n",["$","li",null,{"children":["\n",["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Data Sharding and Redundancy"}],"\nDivide original data into ",["$","code",null,{"children":"k"}]," shards, generate ",["$","code",null,{"children":"m"}]," parity shards (total ",["$","code",null,{"children":"n=k+m"}],"). Any loss of ≤ ",["$","code",null,{"children":"m"}]," shards can recover data.\n",["$","strong",null,{"children":"Example"}],": RS(10,4) strategy allows simultaneous loss of 4 nodes (storage utilization 71%), saving 50% storage space compared to triple replication (33%)."]}],"\n"]}],"\n",["$","li",null,{"children":["\n",["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Fault Recovery Mechanism"}],"\nThrough ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"Gaussian elimination"}]," or ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"Fast Fourier Transform (FFT)"}]," algorithms, use surviving shards to reconstruct lost data, with recovery time inversely proportional to network bandwidth."]}],"\n"]}],"\n","$L13","\n"]}],"\n","$L14","\n","$L15","\n","$L16","\n","$L17","\n","$L18","\n","$L19","\n","$L1a","\n","$L1b","\n","$L1c","\n","$L1d","\n","$L1e","\n","$L1f","\n","$L20","\n","$L21","\n","$L22","\n","$L23","\n","$L24","\n","$L25","\n","$L26","\n","$L27","\n","$L28","\n","$L29","\n","$L2a","\n","$L2b","\n","$L2c","\n","$L2d","\n","$L2e","\n","$L2f","\n","$L30","\n","$L31","\n","$L32","\n","$L33","\n","$L34","\n","$L35"]}],"$undefined"]}]
36:I["327bb606fa81",[],"CodeBlock",1]
43:I["d29067c5123b",[],"Mermaid",1]
13:["$","li",null,{"children":["\n",["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Dynamic Adjustment Capability"}],"\nSupports runtime adjustment of ",["$","code",null,{"children":"(k,m)"}]," parameters to adapt to different storage tiers' (hot/warm/cold data) reliability requirements."]}],"\n"]}]
14:["$","$L12",null,{"as":"h3","id":"12-communication-transmission","children":"1.2. Communication Transmission"}]
15:["$","ul",null,{"children":["\n",["$","li",null,{"children":["\n",["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Satellite Communication"}],"\nHandle long delay, high bit error rate issues in deep space channels (e.g., NASA Mars rover uses RS(255,223) code with error correction capability up to 16 bytes/codeword)."]}],"\n"]}],"\n",["$","li",null,{"children":["\n",["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"5G NR Standards"}],"\nUse RS codes combined with CRC checks in control channels to ensure reliable transmission of critical signaling."]}],"\n"]}],"\n",["$","li",null,{"children":["\n",["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Wireless Sensor Networks"}],"\nSolve cumulative packet loss problems in multi-hop transmission, typical configuration RS(6,2) can tolerate 33% data loss."]}],"\n"]}],"\n"]}]
16:["$","$L12",null,{"as":"h3","id":"13-digital-media-storage","children":"1.3. Digital Media Storage"}]
17:["$","ul",null,{"children":["\n",["$","li",null,{"children":["\n",["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"QR Codes"}],"\nUse RS codes to implement fault tolerance level adjustment (L7%, M15%, Q25%, H30%), ensuring correct decoding even with partially damaged areas."]}],"\n"]}],"\n",["$","li",null,{"children":["\n",["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Blu-ray Discs"}],"\nUse RS(248,216) code combined with cross-interleaving to correct continuous burst errors caused by scratches."]}],"\n"]}],"\n",["$","li",null,{"children":["\n",["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"DNA Data Storage"}],"\nAdd RS checksums during synthetic biomolecular chain synthesis to resist base synthesis/sequencing errors (e.g., Microsoft experimental project uses RS(4,2))."]}],"\n"]}],"\n"]}]
18:["$","$L12",null,{"as":"h2","id":"basic-concepts-of-erasure-coding","children":"Basic Concepts of Erasure Coding"}]
19:["$","$L12",null,{"as":"h3","id":"21-evolution-of-storage-redundancy","children":"2.1 Evolution of Storage Redundancy"}]
37:T1068,<svg viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path d="M23.8346 11.7033l-1.0073-.6236a13.7268 13.7268 0 00-.0283-.2936l.8656-.8069a.3483.3483 0 00-.1154-.578l-1.1066-.414a8.4958 8.4958 0 00-.087-.2856l.6904-.9587a.3462.3462 0 00-.2257-.5446l-1.1663-.1894a9.3574 9.3574 0 00-.1407-.2622l.49-1.0761a.3437.3437 0 00-.0274-.3361.3486.3486 0 00-.3006-.154l-1.1845.0416a6.7444 6.7444 0 1b:["$","p",null,{"children":"Traditional multi-replication schemes have low storage efficiency issues (storage utilization 33%). Erasure coding technology divides data into blocks and calculates checksum information, achieving a balance between storage efficiency and reliability."}]

34:["$","$L12",null,{"as":"h3","id":"43-decoding-recovery-process","children":"4.3 Decoding Recovery Process"}]
35:["$","$L43",null,{"chart":"sequenceDiagram\n Client->>Coordinator: Data read request\n Coordinator->>Nodes: Query shard status\n alt Sufficient available shards\n Nodes->>Coordinator: Return k shards\n Coordinator->>Decoder: Start decoding\n Decoder->>Client: Return original data\n else Insufficient shards\n Coordinator->>Repairer: Trigger repair process\n Repairer->>Nodes: Collect surviving shards\n Repairer->>Decoder: Data reconstruction\n Decoder->>Nodes: Write new shards\n end"}]
